title: Nvidia Earnings Preview: Here's what Wall Street expects
text: Nvidia is reporting first-quarter fiscal earnings with Wall Street anticipating some growth deceleration following exceptional performance in prior quarters. Analyst Consensus Forecasts: Earnings per share: $5.22 (up 474%), Revenue: $24.5 billion (up 241%). These figures represent a slowdown from the previous quarter, which saw a 765% earnings jump and 265% revenue surge. Nvidia's stock has surged 87% year-to-date and approximately 200% over the past year. The company now ranks as the world's third most valuable enterprise with a $2.3 trillion market capitalization, trailing only Apple and Microsoft. Bank of America flagged concerns about the transition from H100 chips to the new Blackwell generation, noting that the first time sequential growth will be less than 10%. Additionally, gross margins are projected to decline from approximately 77% to a normalized 75%-76%. Despite potential headwinds, Bank of America maintains a bullish stance with a buy rating and $1,100 price target, suggesting 19% upside potential. Morgan Stanley similarly remains optimistic, citing strong underlying AI spending demand and Nvidia's competitive advantages.
